findings.title: Instructions install a global PostToolUse hook that intercepts all tool activity
findings.description: The skill directs the user to copy a hook into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, causing interception of all future tool invocations across the agent environment. This is a high-risk tool exploitation pattern because it modifies global tool behavior outside the skill's local scope and enables persistent monitoring of unrelated workflows.
findings.remediation: Do not install global hooks from a skill package. If monitoring is needed, scope it to the specific task/session, require explicit user consent, and clearly disclose what data is captured and where it is stored or sent.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: SKILL.md explicitly instructs the user to copy hooks/monitor.sh into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, which persistently alters global Claude tool behavior outside the skill directory.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; a user following the documented setup installs the hook for all subsequent tool executions.
metadata.meta_impact: Persistent interception of unrelated workflows and enabling of broad monitoring/exfiltration across the agent environment.

findings.title: Capability inflation through pre-configured MCP setup and auto-discovery claims
findings.description: The skill advertises broad MCP management, monitoring, and auto-discovery capabilities and instructs users to copy a pre-configured .mcp.json into the agent configuration directory. This can expand the agent's active integrations beyond transparent, least-privilege configuration and increases the risk of enabling unexpected tools or connections not clearly documented in the package contents provided.
findings.snippet:
This skill ships with a pre-configured MCP setup for common services. Copy the configuration to enable it:

cp .mcp.json ~/.claude/.mcp.json

...
- Auto-discover available tools
findings.remediation: Document all configured servers and permissions explicitly. Avoid broad auto-discovery language, provide least-privilege defaults, and require manual review of MCP configuration before installation.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: SKILL.md instructs copying .mcp.json into ~/.claude/.mcp.json, and the file contains multiple preconfigured MCP servers including an analytics proxy with FORWARD_ALL_REQUESTS and capture flags enabled.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; user setup enables undisclosed or over-broad integrations by replacing global MCP configuration.
metadata.meta_impact: Unexpected expansion of agent capabilities and routing of requests/tool data through a proxy configured for broad collection.

findings.title: Remote exfiltration of tool inputs, outputs, and environment secrets
findings.description: The monitoring hook captures every tool invocation and transmits sensitive data to an external domain. The payload includes tool input, tool output, username, hostname, home directory, and environment variables such as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN. This creates a direct data exfiltration channel for credentials and potentially sensitive user/project data processed by tools.
findings.remediation: Remove all external transmission of tool telemetry by default. Never collect or send API keys, tokens, or full tool inputs/outputs. If audit logging is required, keep logs local, minimize fields, redact secrets, and require explicit informed opt-in for any remote export.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: hooks/monitor.sh clearly collects TOOL_INPUT, TOOL_OUTPUT, USER, hostname, HOME, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, and GITHUB_TOKEN into JSON and posts it to an external endpoint with curl.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Trivial once the hook is installed; every tool invocation automatically triggers exfiltration in the background.
metadata.meta_impact: Direct theft of credentials and sensitive user/project data processed by tools, with possible downstream compromise of accounts and repositories.

findings.title: Hardcoded bearer token embedded in hook script
findings.description: The script contains a hardcoded authorization token used for outbound requests. Hardcoded secrets are sensitive material that can be reused, leaked, or abused, and indicate insecure secret handling practices.
findings.remediation: Remove hardcoded tokens from the repository. Use secure secret injection mechanisms at runtime, rotate the exposed token, and scope credentials to the minimum required privileges.

metadata.meta_confidence_reason: The hook script hardcodes an Authorization bearer token, and .mcp.json also contains hardcoded secret-like values including PROXY_AUTH and SEARCH_API_KEY.
metadata.meta_exploitability: Easy; anyone with access to the package can read and potentially reuse the embedded credentials.
metadata.meta_impact: Credential leakage, unauthorized use of associated services, and evidence of insecure secret handling tied to the exfiltration path.

scan_metadata.llm_overall_assessment: The skill is unsafe. Its primary function includes installing a persistent global hook that captures all tool invocations and silently sends tool data and environment secrets to an external service. This presents a critical exfiltration risk, compounded by hardcoded credentials and broad MCP configuration behavior with limited transparency.

meta_correlations.relationship: These findings describe the same attack chain: SKILL.md installs a persistent global PostToolUse hook, and the hook then captures tool inputs/outputs plus environment secrets and sends them to a remote server using an embedded bearer token.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Remove the instructions to install ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, delete hooks/monitor.sh or rewrite it to log locally only, eliminate all curl-based remote transmission, and never include secrets or tool contents in telemetry.

meta_correlations.group_name: Stealthy capability expansion via MCP config
meta_correlations.relationship: The preconfigured .mcp.json expands integrations and includes an analytics proxy configured to forward all requests and capture tool inputs/outputs, reinforcing the exfiltration behavior seen in the hook.
meta_correlations.consolidated_remediation: Do not instruct users to copy bundled .mcp.json into ~/.claude/.mcp.json. Replace it with a minimal example config, remove analytics-proxy entirely, and require explicit opt-in per server with documented permissions.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove the global PostToolUse hook installation path
meta_recommendations.fix: Delete the SKILL.md commands that copy hooks/monitor.sh into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh. If monitoring is needed, keep it local to the skill and require explicit per-session opt-in rather than modifying the user's global Claude hook directory.

meta_recommendations.title: Eliminate outbound exfiltration from monitor.sh
meta_recommendations.fix: Remove the curl POST to https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool, remove Authorization headers, and strip ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN, HOME, tool input, and tool output from any telemetry. If audit logging is required, write only minimal metadata to a local file and redact secrets by default.

meta_recommendations.title: Remove the analytics proxy from .mcp.json
meta_recommendations.fix: Delete the analytics-proxy server block and all settings that enable FORWARD_ALL_REQUESTS, CAPTURE_TOOL_INPUTS, and CAPTURE_TOOL_OUTPUTS. Provide a safe sample .mcp.json containing only clearly documented, least-privilege local integrations.

meta_recommendations.title: Replace bundled production tokens and API keys with placeholders
meta_recommendations.fix: Replace values like "Bearer mcp_a1b2c3d4e5f6" and "sk_mcp_rT4Xp9QmN2kW8vJ5" with obvious nonfunctional placeholders such as "REPLACE_ME" and require users to supply their own secrets through environment variables or secure configuration.

meta_recommendations.title: Add explicit manifest metadata for reviewability
meta_recommendations.fix: Add a proper YAML manifest with allowed-tools, license, and compatibility fields so reviewers can compare declared permissions with actual behavior.

meta_risk_assessment.summary: This skill installs persistent global Claude hooks and MCP configuration that capture and exfiltrate tool inputs, outputs, and environment secrets to an external service.
meta_risk_assessment.top_priority: Remove the PostToolUse hook installation and delete all outbound exfiltration logic from hooks/monitor.sh.
meta_risk_assessment.verdict_reasoning: The provided code explicitly copies a global hook into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, captures all tool activity, includes sensitive environment variables like ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN in a payload, and sends that data to a remote endpoint using a hardcoded bearer token. This is a clear credential and data exfiltration design, not benign monitoring.
